**Network City** - Learn Cisco networking by running a city, not memorizing commands.

Delivery vehicles now spawn on a repeating 5-second interval instead of a single one-time vehicle, and IOS 'shutdown'/'no shutdown' commands now actually block and resume delivery traffic through the interface they're run on.

What shipped in "Recurring Delivery Traffic and Interface Administrative Shutdown":

- Recurring delivery vehicle spawning every 5 simulated seconds via a new DeliverySpawnSystem in the simulation engine
- Unique sequential delivery vehicle ids (delivery-1, delivery-2, delivery-3, ...)
- Automatic removal of a delivery vehicle from the active vehicle list once it arrives, so the vehicle list does not grow without bound during normal (non-blocked) operation
- IOS CLI 'shutdown' and 'no shutdown' commands in interface configuration mode, which set NetworkInterface.administrativeStatus
- DeliverySystem now also checks each link endpoint's operationalStatus in addition to link.status, so an administratively shut down interface blocks delivery vehicles from entering that link

What you'd notice if you were using it:

- A new delivery vehicle appears roughly every 5 seconds and follows the House -> R1 -> R2 -> Hospital route
- Multiple delivery vehicles can be on screen and in transit at the same time
- Typing 'shutdown' on a router interface in the CLI now visibly stops delivery vehicles from crossing that interface's link
- Typing 'no shutdown' lets queued-up delivery vehicles resume and continue their route

What's still missing / known limitations:

- Vehicles still accumulate without bound while a link/interface stays down indefinitely (a congestion queue at the blocked node) - only steady-state, non-blocked operation is guaranteed to stay bounded
- Movement speed (120 world units/second) was kept as-is from the prior milestone rather than tuned for readability with multiple simultaneous vehicles on screen
- No new UI controls were added; shutdown/no shutdown are CLI-only, and delivery vehicles are rendered as plain colored dots, not sprites

Repo: https://github.com/lddorse/network-city
